title: Decomposition
subtitle: Break Big Problems into Small Parts!
overview: This session introduces students to the concept of Decomposition — breaking a big problem into smaller, simpler parts to make it easier to understand and solve.

images._note: Add classroom activity photos, chart paper activities, coding tasks, and teamwork images.

realLifeExamples.title: School Bag Packing
realLifeExamples.example: Packing a school bag can be divided into books, lunchbox, bottle, notebook and stationery.

realLifeExamples.title: Cleaning a Room
realLifeExamples.example: Cleaning includes arranging books, sweeping floor, folding clothes and cleaning table.

realLifeExamples.title: Coding a Game
realLifeExamples.example: Game development can be divided into character design, movement, scoring and sounds.

options: Breaking a big problem into smaller parts
quiz.explanation: Decomposition means dividing a big task into smaller, easier tasks.
Breaking problems into smaller parts helps us solve them more easily.

quiz.q: Which is an example of decomposition?
quiz.explanation: Making a sandwich requires dividing the work into simple steps.

quiz.q: In coding, decomposition helps programmers to:
quiz.explanation: Programmers divide big programs into smaller manageable parts.
Many daily activities can be divided into smaller tasks.
The first step is dividing the big problem into smaller parts.
Decomposition develops logical and organized thinking.

quiz.q: Which of these is NOT decomposition?

options: Different people can handle different tasks
quiz.explanation: Tasks can be divided among team members for better efficiency.
Decomposition is useful in many subjects and real-life situations.
